title: ANS sealed identity event (V2)
description: Conformance schema for a sealed Verified-Identity Transparency-Log entry (the IDENTITY_* event family). Mirrors ANS-0 §8.1 and the producer payload the RA POSTs to POST /v1/internal/identities/event. Sealed shapes are append-only-forever: the five eventType tokens, the field set, and the per-type required-field matrix are the wire contract. The strict, conformance-critical constraints live on IdentityEvent (the producer-authored, JCS-canonicalized inner event); the envelope status is TL-assigned and intentionally permissive here. NOTE: the keys[]/ProvenKey shape below is the verbatim-verification-method seal used by the JWS-scheme kinds (did:web, did:key). The postponed `lei` kind will seal a thumbprint-only variant (subject AID + key thumbprint, no verificationMethod) — that is a future schema amendment, not covered by this revision.

schemaVersion.description: Envelope version. Identity events are part of the V2 surface; the V1 lane never carries them.

signature.description: TL attestation over the sealed entry (detached JWS).

status.description: TL-assigned envelope status. Not constrained here; the authoritative, computed identity status is derived at read time (ANS-0 §8.2–§8.3).

keyId.description: Producer key id in the TL producer-key registry.

signature.description: RA producer attestation over the JCS-canonical inner event (detached JWS).

IdentityEvent.description: The producer-authored inner identity event. JCS-canonicalized and signed by the RA; keyed by identityId.

providerId.description: The owning principal — the who's owner.

keys.description: The proven key set; one entry per key the registrant proved possession of. Required (non-empty) on the proof events.

ansIds.description: Linked agents' ids — the whole batch in one event (IDENTITY_LINKED / IDENTITY_UNLINKED only).

allOf.comment: Proof events carry the proven key set, verifiedAt, and providerId.
Link events carry a non-empty ansIds batch.
Non-link events never name agents: ansIds is absent or empty.

ProvenKey.description: One key the registrant proved possession of, sealed self-verifyingly. The JWS-scheme (did:web, did:key) seal shape.

verificationMethod.description: The verification method quoted EXACTLY as the source published it (id, type, controller, and the key material in publicKeyJwk or publicKeyMultibase) — member-for-member, values untouched. Additional members are preserved verbatim. At least one key-material member MUST be present so the seal is checkable from the badge alone (ANS-0 §8.4).

signedProof.description: The compact JWS the registrant submitted over the served IdentityProofInput (its payload segment equals the served signingInput verbatim).
